items.title: AP top stories July 19
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Here's the latest for Saturday, July 19th: Car struck crowd outside Los Angeles club; Syria’s interim president urges factions to commit to ceasefire; Search continues after thunderstorm capsizes tourist boat; Valenzuela release jailed Americans

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: Tourists in awe of spectacular lava action as Iceland volcanic eruption reduces in intensity
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Tourists have been allowed back to Iceland's Reykjanes Peninsula to witness the magic of the ongoing volcanic eruptions. Lava continued to bubble in the Sundhnúksgígar crater row early on Saturday, to the delight of those who made it to the site. 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Latest at the scene in East Hollywood after car drove into nightclub crowd
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The scene outside The Vermont Hollywood nightclub where a car drove into a crowd outside the club early Saturday morning, injuring 30 people.

items.title: Bangladesh’s largest Islamist party projects growing influence with mass rally in Dhaka
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Hundreds of thousands of supporters of Bangladesh’s largest Islamist party rallied in the capital on Saturday to show their strength ahead of elections expected next year, as the South Asian nation stands at a crossroads after the ouster of former Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina. Ahead of the march, Jamaat-e-Islami said it aimed to mobilize 1 million people on Saturday. (AP video/Al Emrun Garjon)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Maduro celebrates return of Venezuelans held in El Salvador maximum security prison
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro appeared triumphant on Friday, celebrating the return to Venezuela of migrants held in El Salvador after being deported from the U.S., after Venezuela released 10 jailed Americans on Friday in exchange. (AP video/Andry Rincon and Juan Arraez)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.author: Associated Press

<article>More explosions and plumes of smoke were seen in the Gaza Strip on Saturday as Israel continued its military operation.

items.title: Iconic tiny Tokyo capsule home goes on display in New York
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The stacks of boxes with their distinctive circular windows were a local landmark in the Ginza District. Each was a 'micro-dwelling', a tiny home just about big enough for one. In 2022, the building was demolished - but it wasn't the end for all its mini homes. Of the 140 single-occupancy capsules, 14 have been salvaged, including this one.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Los Angeles fire official says 30 people injured after vehicle drove into crowd outside nightclub
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A vehicle rammed into a crowd of people waiting to enter a nightclub along a busy boulevard in Los Angeles early Saturday, injuring 30 people.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: Tense scenes in Sweida as Druze-Bedouin clashes threaten to undermine Syria's fragile peace
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Renewed violence in Syria between Druze militias and Bedouin tribes is threatening to deepen divisions in a country recuperating from a civil war. Arab tribal fighters from all over Syria have travelled to the mainly Druze area of Sweida province to help support their Bedouin kin.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Bodies brought to Gaza hospital after scores killed trying to reach food distribution sites
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Israeli troops opened fire Saturday toward crowds of Palestinians seeking food from distribution hubs run by a U.S.-Israeli-backed group in southern Gaza, killing at least 32 people, according to witnesses and hospital officials. (AP video/Mariam Dagga, Production/Wafaa Shurafa)


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Scores dead or missing as tourist boat capsizes during thunderstorm in Vietnam
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>At least 34 people were killed and eight were missing after a tourist boat capsized in bad weather in Vietnam on Saturday, state media reported. The Wonder Sea boat was carrying 48 passengers and five crew members for a sightseeing tour of Ha Long Bay, a popular destination for visitors.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: ‘Love Island’ star Kaylor Martin on reuniting with Aaron Evans
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>“Love Island USA” season 6 star Kaylor Martin reunites with Aaron Evans on the new spinoff “Love Island: Beyond the Villa.” Martin says returning together gave them a chance to reconnect after their time in the villa. 
#loveisland #loveislandbeyondthevilla #shorts</article>

items.title: US ambassador Huckabee visits West Bank village after Israeli settlers attack ancient church
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>U.S. Ambassador to Israel Mike Huckabee visited a Palestinian Christian village in the West Bank on Saturday where residents say extremist Israeli settlers set fire to the ancient Church of St. George on 9 July. (AP video/Imad Isseid)
